notes: All 17 paywall modal tests PASS — `npx jest __tests__/features/paywall/QuotaUpsellModal.test.tsx` → 'Tests: 17 passed, 17 total'. The 12 R1/F2 baseline tests survive unchanged + 5 new F3 tests green (F3.A raw-ISO-absent, F3.B EN human, F3.C FR human, F3.D invalid-ISO fallback, F3.E memo stability).

FE regression scope: `npx jest __tests__/features/paywall __tests__/shared/api/paywall-interceptor __tests__/infrastructure/httpClient.test.ts __tests__/features/auth` → 7 suites, 71 passed (spec required ≥71). Met exactly.

BE regression: `pnpm test -- --testPathPattern='(chat-session-quota-ordering|monthlySessionQuota|leads)' --no-coverage` → 10 suites, 75 passed + 1 todo (spec required ≥75). Met. F3 has zero BE surface, clean.

EN locale renders 'June 1, 2026' (regex /June.*2026/), FR renders '1 juin 2026' (regex /juin.*2026/) — both human-readable per F3.R1. Raw ISO regex `/2026-06-01T00:00:00\.000Z/` returns 0 hits (F3.A pins absence).

Defensive fallback at line 119-122 — `Number.isNaN(date.getTime())` short-circuits BEFORE the `Intl.DateTimeFormat(...).format(date)` call. This is a SAFER pattern than the spec's recommended bare try/catch: invalid Date instances don't even reach `.format()`, so 'Invalid Date' string can never leak. F3.D test asserts both raw fallback rendered AND 'Invalid Date' string absent — both pass.

MINOR — Spec §5 T1 enumerated 5 tests F3.A-E with specific coverage : EN happy (AC1), FR happy (AC2), invalid-ISO fallback (AC3), LOCALE-CHANGE RE-RENDER mid-modal (AC5), F2 COMPOSE on visible cycle (AC7). Implementation renamed tests to A-E but swapped coverage: spec's AC5 (locale change via `i18n.changeLanguage` while modal mounted) is NOT implemented at all, and spec's AC7 (F3+F2 compose verifying email reset PRESERVES formatted date across visible cycle) is NOT implemented — replaced by a memo-stability smoke test (output-equality re-render). The locale-change runtime path is theoretically exercised by F3.B (EN) + F3.C (FR) using separate renders with different mock language, but the in-flight `useTranslation` re-render contract on `changeLanguage` is NOT pinned. F3.R5 mechanically unverified in CI.

MINOR — Spec §5 T1 also enumerated F3.AC6 (spy on `Intl.DateTimeFormat` constructor via `jest.spyOn(global.Intl, 'DateTimeFormat')`) to assert ≤2 calls across 5 email keystrokes. Implementation replaced this with F3.E output-equality smoke — verifies formatted text is byte-identical across re-renders. The memo deps shape `[resetAtIso, i18n.language]` is sound by inspection, but the per-render constructor-invocation count is NOT mechanically pinned. F3.N5 (memoization NFR) verified by code-review only, not by CI gate.

T3 manual smoke on real Hermes device (per F3.AC12) NOT performed — green-agent honestly flagged this as required pre-merge but did not execute. Risk1 (Hermes Intl unavailable on dev device → all 8 locales see raw ISO) remains unverified runtime-wise. Acceptable defer if T3 smoke is gated as a pre-merge step ; concerning if the loop closes without it.

Diff scope = 4 files, all within F3 scope-IN per spec §0.3: CLAUDE.md (+1 doctrine bullet), F3.md (+449 spec file), QuotaUpsellModal.tsx (+35 / -3), QuotaUpsellModal.test.tsx (+132). `git diff 3cdebfe8~1 3cdebfe8 --stat` confirms exactly 4 files, +617 / -3.

Zero drift into PaywallProvider.tsx, app/_layout.tsx, paywall-interceptor.ts, httpClient.ts. The 402 wire contract `{ tier, currentCount, limit, resetAt: string }` byte-unchanged at the boundary — type stays string-ISO, format applied only at render time. Spec §0.4 scope-OUT honored exactly.
Zero BE surface — no museum-backend file touched. The monthly-session-quota.middleware.ts `firstOfNextUtcMonthIso()` emitter (R1 AC3 integration test pins this) untouched. Wire format byte-unchanged.
Sibling R-series files (R2/R3/R4/R5 territory) byte-unchanged. F1 surface (chat-session.route.ts middleware order) untouched. F2's useEffect at lines 89-98 is byte-identical to the F2 commit.
Tracker flip (ultrareview-fixes.md F3 pending→closed) correctly deferred to verify-agent per spec §0.3 last bullet — not green-code-agent's responsibility this commit.

Minimal diff to production code: 1 import line addition (`useEffect, useState` → `useEffect, useMemo, useState`), 1 destructure addition (`{ t }` → `{ t, i18n }`), 1 JSX swap (`{reason.resetAt}` → `{formattedReset}`), and a 16-line memo block (lines 100-130) with 14 lines of explanatory comment. Total: ~35 lines added.

useMemo dep array `[resetAtIso, i18n.language]` matches the only two render-time inputs (the ISO date string and the active locale). Memo fires on prop change OR locale change, stable across email/consent/state changes. Correct.

resetAtIso primitive lift (line 114) is a FORCED workaround for React Compiler's `preserve-manual-memoization` rule, NOT a stylistic choice. I independently verified by writing a probe file with the spec's example shape `[reason?.resetAt, i18n.language]` and running ESLint → 'Could not preserve existing manual memoization' error fires at the deps array. The lifted-primitive shape `[resetAtIso, i18n.language]` passes cleanly. Honest workaround. The inline comment (lines 108-113) accurately documents WHY: the compiler infers `reason` (object-level) as the broader dep when an optional chain is used in deps array, breaking granularity guarantees.

Defensive `Number.isNaN(date.getTime())` check (line 119) is a stronger pattern than the spec's recommended bare try/catch. The spec recommended wrapping the entire `new Intl.DateTimeFormat(...).format(...)` in try/catch ; the implementation short-circuits invalid Date BEFORE the format call, which avoids the documented JS quirk that `.format(NaN_Date)` THROWS RangeError in some ICU versions and returns 'Invalid Date' in others. The try/catch is preserved as a secondary net for the rare 'Intl unsupported locale' RangeError. Defense-in-depth, no over-engineering.

CLAUDE.md doctrine bullet (line 150) is concise + actionable: names the failure pattern (`<Text>{isoString}</Text>` anti-pattern with the exact ugly output), the canonical fix (useMemo + Intl.DateTimeFormat keyed on `[iso, locale]` + try/catch), and the canonical example. Slightly compressed vs spec template (drops the explicit `dateStyle: 'long'` recommendation and the Hermes ICU support note) but the gist transfers.

MINOR — The inline comment block (lines 100-113) is 14 lines (including the React Compiler workaround paragraph). This is heavier than F1/F2's comment blocks (~10 lines each). Justified by the non-obvious deviation but pushes the comment-to-code ratio toward 1:1. Future readers benefit from the verbose justification, though, so this is a soft observation rather than a refactor request.

MINOR — F3.D test's invalid-ISO fixture `'NOT-A-VALID-DATE'` uses uppercase, but the test grep at line 415 (`queryAllByText(/NOT-A-VALID-DATE/)`) is case-sensitive. Test passes only because the fallback returns the input verbatim. Tests do NOT cover the empty-string fallback case (spec F3.AC4 — `resetAt: ''` → metadata line shows 'Resets on ' with trailing space). The component handles this correctly via the `!resetAtIso` short-circuit (line 116) but no test pins the behavior.

Date format respects locale via `Intl.DateTimeFormat(i18n.language, { dateStyle: 'long' })` — EN renders 'June 1, 2026', FR renders '1 juin 2026'. Word-ordering automatic per ICU locale data. Numerals locale-aware: Arabic locale would render Arabic-Indic digits by default (`numberingSystem` not overridden — F3 Q3 default position is to let Intl's locale convention apply, which is locale-faithful).

Metadata line styles (`styles.meta` at line 283-287 — fontSize 12, opacity 0.7) byte-unchanged. The localized 'June 1, 2026' or '1 juin 2026' renders at the same size + opacity as the prior raw ISO. No new layout / contrast concerns. Long FR locales (e.g. '15 décembre 2026' = 17 chars vs '2026-06-01T00:00:00.000Z' = 24 chars) are SHORTER than the prior raw render, so no truncation risk introduced.

Modal `accessibilityRole='alert'` (line 160) byte-unchanged. R1 R26 a11y tests pass unchanged. Email field `accessibilityLabel`, consent checkbox `accessibilityState`, close button `accessibilityLabel` — all byte-unchanged. F3 is render-text-only.

MINOR — `dateStyle: 'long'` is the spec's pick per Q1 default position, but the spec's verbatim example in R1 §3.4 D4 reads `"Resets on June 1"` (year OMITTED). Implementation includes the year ('June 1, 2026'). Spec Q1 explicitly chose to include year for cross-year disambiguation (Dec→Jan reset case). Defensible UX choice, but ICU formatting may render differently across locales for the same `dateStyle: 'long'` (e.g. en-GB might yield '1 June 2026'). F3.B test uses permissive regex `/June.*2026/` which covers both orderings.

MINOR — Arabic + RTL behavior NOT directly tested. F3.B (EN) + F3.C (FR) cover only LTR locales. Arabic users will see Arabic-Indic digits per Intl default (locale-faithful) and RTL-ordered date components — the codebase has RTL doctrine (`feedback_rtl_start_end` memory) but no F3 test pins the RTL render. Risk7 acknowledged in spec.

Defensive fallback surfaces BE drift visibly to QA (per F3.R3 + spec §3.3). The `Number.isNaN(date.getTime())` branch returns the raw ISO string, so a malformed BE payload renders as 'Resets on garbage-string' — ugly but informative. NOT silent empty string (would hide the bug), NOT 'Invalid Date' string (would mislead). Honest degradation path. F3.D test pins this.

T3 manual smoke (Hermes runtime Intl probe) honestly flagged as REQUIRED PRE-MERGE in the green-agent's acknowledgments + in spec §3.5 + Risk1. The commit body explicitly admits 'Hermes Intl NOT runtime-verified — Jest = Node Intl ≠ Hermes Intl'. The defensive try/catch is the safety net IF Hermes-on-device strips Intl ; T3 is the runtime gate that converts documented-availability into runtime-verified. Honest framing per UFR-013.

F3 honestly implements R1 §3.4 D4 intent (no supersession needed). Spec §Appendix A line 440 verbatim: 'F3 implements R1's intent, not supersedes it'. The R1 design statement was correct ; the R1 green-agent just didn't deliver. F3 closes the gap. Truthful framing — contrasts with F1 which DID supersede R1 §3.3 D3 middleware order.

resetAtIso primitive lift HONESTLY documented as a React Compiler workaround in the inline comment (lines 108-113). The comment explicitly says 'lifting `resetAt` to a primitive const lets the compiler and our memo agree on the same granular key'. The commit body also flags this: 'resetAtIso primitive const lifted out of memo to satisfy React Compiler preserve-manual-memoization without weakening to [reason, lang]'. I independently verified the workaround is real, not invented — the probe (spec's recommended `[reason?.resetAt, i18n.language]` shape) triggers `react-hooks/preserve-manual-memoization` error in ESLint. LEGITIMATE workaround.
No new feature flag — `feedback_no_feature_flags_prelaunch` honored. No `INTL_DATE_FORMAT_ENABLED` toggle introduced (F3.N1 closed). No new env var, no new dep (Intl is JS built-in — F3.N4 closed).

MINOR HONESTY NIT — The acknowledgment in the spec-message text states 'defensive try/catch + Number.isNaN check covers the worst case'. Implementation goes BEYOND the spec's recommended pattern by adding the explicit `Number.isNaN(date.getTime())` short-circuit (line 119-122), which is a STRONGER guarantee than the bare try/catch the spec recommends. Improvement, but worth flagging that the agent silently strengthened the spec — would have been more transparent to call this out as a deliberate enhancement in the commit body.

MINOR HONESTY NIT — The spec §5 T1.4 mandated a locale-change-during-modal-open test using `i18n.changeLanguage('fr')` wrapped in `act()` (F3.AC5). The implementation did NOT deliver this test — instead implemented F3.D (defensive fallback) + F3.E (memo stability smoke). The commit body doesn't flag the spec deviation. A future reader scanning F3.AC5 vs the test file will find no matching test. F3.R5 (memo re-renders on locale change) is functionally exercised by F3.B/F3.C using separate renders with different mock language, but the in-flight `useTranslation` `changeLanguage` re-render flow is NOT pinned in CI. Soft test-coverage gap, not pinning, not honestly disclosed.

niceToHave: Implement spec F3.AC5 (locale-change-during-modal-open) — render with mock language 'en', assert 'June 1, 2026' visible, mutate mock language to 'fr' via the `setLanguage` helper, force re-render (rerender with new props or fire a no-op fireEvent), assert '1 juin 2026' visible + 'June 1, 2026' absent. Pins F3.R5 in CI. ~15 LoC.

Implement spec F3.AC7 (F3 + F2 compose) — render visible=true, type email, tick consent, assert formatted date visible + email value set + consent ticked. Cycle visible→false→true. Assert email reset to '' (F2.A invariant) + consent unchecked (F2.B invariant) + formatted date STILL visible (F3 stable). Pins the orthogonality between F3's memo deps and F2's effect deps. ~25 LoC.

Add F3.AC4 (empty-string resetAt fallback) — fixture `resetAt: ''` → assert metadata line still renders (because `reason !== null`) AND `formattedReset === ''` so the rendered text is 'Resets on ' (with trailing space, no crash). The component handles this correctly via the `!resetAtIso` short-circuit at line 116 but no test pins it. ~10 LoC.

Execute T3 manual smoke on real Hermes device per F3.AC12 BEFORE merging — run dev build (Expo Go or EAS dev client), trigger 402 × 4 in EN, assert metadata reads 'Resets on June 1, 2026' (not raw ISO). Switch device locale to FR, re-trigger, assert 'Réinitialisation le 1 juin 2026'. If smoke fails → STOP, escalate per Risk1.

Expand CLAUDE.md doctrine bullet to include the explicit `dateStyle: 'long'` recommendation + the Hermes ICU support context. Current bullet covers the WHAT (useMemo + Intl.DateTimeFormat + try/catch) but not the WHY-this-style. A future dev hitting the same pattern class won't know whether to use 'long', 'medium', 'short' without re-reading F3.md. ~2 sentences extension.

Add TECH_DEBT.md entry per spec Q5 default-YES — 'F3 follow-up: audit other ISO-in-UI render sites across museum-frontend with grep `(\.toISOString\(\)|\.resetAt|\.createdAt|\.scheduledAt)\}`'. Cheap, prevents the doctrine bullet from being a one-off note.

resetAtIsoDeviationVerdict.rationale: I independently verified the workaround by writing a probe component with the spec's recommended deps shape `[reason?.resetAt, i18n.language]` and running `npx eslint` on it. The output: `react-hooks/preserve-manual-memoization` error fires at the deps array (`Could not preserve existing manual memoization`). The rule comes from `eslint-plugin-react-hooks@7.0.1` (React Compiler era) which is loaded via `reactHooks.configs.flat.recommended` in `museum-frontend/eslint.config.mjs:64`. The lifted-primitive shape `[resetAtIso, i18n.language]` passes cleanly (probe with the impl shape → 0 errors). The compiler's behavior is documented: it infers the broader `reason` object as the dep when an optional chain accesses a nested field in the deps array, which would force re-format on every PaywallProvider state emission (reference-instability per spec §3.4 D4). The lift to a primitive const preserves the spec's granular memoization intent without weakening to `[reason, i18n.language]`. The inline comment block (lines 108-113) honestly documents the deviation. The commit body explicitly calls it out. UFR-013 transparency exemplary.

hermesIntlVerification.rationale: The implementation has a TWO-LAYER defensive fallback (Number.isNaN short-circuit + outer try/catch on lines 117-129) that would render the raw ISO if Hermes Intl is unavailable. Worst-case behavior on a Hermes-without-Intl device = pre-fix behavior (raw ISO visible). NOT a regression vs baseline. The Jest harness runs against Node Intl (which IS available + ICU-backed), so Jest tests PASS regardless of Hermes runtime support. T3 manual smoke (spec §5 T3 + F3.AC12) is the runtime-verify gate that the green-agent honestly flagged as required pre-merge. The risk is properly framed in spec Risk1 (LOW probability — Hermes 0.14.1 documented to ship ICU DateTimeFormat ; HIGH impact if it materializes). Accepting the defer is honest engineering provided merge-time enforces the T3 smoke.
hermesIntlVerification.ciIntegrationProbeNotes: Adding a CI integration runtime probe would require either (a) a Maestro E2E flow that triggers 402 + visually asserts the formatted date in the rendered modal (heavyweight, days of test infra), or (b) an Expo dev-build CI matrix that boots a minimal harness and probes `typeof Intl.DateTimeFormat` (still requires device farm). Both are out of F3 scope. The pragmatic gate is the merge-blocking T3 smoke step in the green-agent's pre-merge checklist. Recommendation: KEEP T3 as a manual gate, do NOT block F3 on CI integration probe. Promote to V1.1 if Hermes Intl drift becomes a recurring risk class.

honestyVerification.memoShapeVerified: useMemo(() => { if (!resetAtIso) return ''; try { const date = new Date(resetAtIso); if (Number.isNaN(date.getTime())) return resetAtIso; return new Intl.DateTimeFormat(i18n.language, { dateStyle: 'long' }).format(date); } catch { return resetAtIso; } }, [resetAtIso, i18n.language]); — lines 115-130. Primitive `resetAtIso = reason?.resetAt ?? ''` lifted at line 114 satisfies React Compiler preserve-manual-memoization. Defensive Number.isNaN short-circuit + outer try/catch provide defense-in-depth.
